problem: Reverse-engineer 64keys.com chart combination features (interaction, penta, multichart, transits) and build comprehensive Human Design ontology to enable ergonomic multi-chart calculations during Rebecca's consultation sessions

metadata.isolation: automatic git fork and branch creation

researcher.raw_result: AgentRunResult(output={'research_topic': 'Reverse-engineer 64keys.com chart combination features and build comprehensive Human Design ontology', 'focus_areas': 'key concepts and terminology', 'executive_summary': "This Human Design project contains extensive documentation on the core ontology and terminology of Human Design charts. The system is centered around 64 gates (from I Ching hexagrams), 36 channels (gate pairs), 9 centers (energy hubs), circuits/circuit groups, and lines. While the codebase references multi-chart capabilities like 'Penta Force Field' (a 5-person composite) in gate descriptions, there is no explicit implementation or documentation yet for chart combination features like interaction charts, penta, multichart, or transits. The project appears focused on individual chart calculations and memorization pedagogy.", 'key_concepts_and_terminology': {'foundational_structures': {'gates': {'definition': '64 gates corresponding to I Ching hexagrams, each spanning 5.625° (5°37\'30") of the tropical zodiac', 'structure': 'Each gate has 6 lines (subdivisions of 56\'15" each), a name from 64keys naming convention, and belongs to one of 9 centers', 'examples': ['Gate 1 - belong to no circuit, focus on self-empowerment', 'freebie_channels': '3 channels that share their name with one gate: Mutation (3-60), Rhythm (5-15), Judgment (18-58)'}, 'centers': {'definition': '9 energy centers in the BodyGraph, analogous to chakras but with different meanings', 'list': ['Inspiration (Head/Crown) - Self-empowerment, stands alone (6 channels: 10-20, 10-34, 10-57, 20-34, 20-57, 34-57)'], 'characteristics': 'Different, sudden, deaf to others, mutation-focused'}, 'collective': {'theme': "Sharing, patterns, experience - Channels formed by transits + natal\n\n# Historical transit (Rebecca's use case)\nsix_months_ago = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=180)\nhistorical = TransitOverlay(sandy.chart, six_months_ago)\n\n# Compare\nfor channel in today_transit.transit_natal_channels:\n    if channel not in historical.transit_natal_channels:\n        print(f'New channel since 6 months ago: {channel.name}')", 'model_structure': {'TransitOverlay': {'natal_chart': 'RawBodyGraph', 'transit_date': 'datetime', 'transit_activations': 'list[RawActivation] (computed)', 'computed_fields': ['transit_natal_channels: list[Channel] (transit activates natal gate to form channel)', "transit_activated_centers: set[CenterName] (which natal centers are 'lit up')"]}}}, 'group_and_relationship_api': {'python_example': '# Group with relationships\nfrom human_design import Person, Group, Relationship\n\n# Create group\nsandy_group = Group(\n    name=\'Sandy family\',\n    owner=Person.load(\'Sandy Johnson\')\n)\n\n# Add members with relationships\nheath = Person.load(\'Heath Johnson\')\nsandy_group.add_member(heath, relationship=\'husband\')\n\ndaughter = Person.load(\'Emma Johnson\')\nsandy_group.add_member(daughter, relationship=\'daughter\')\n\n# Quick chart access\nfor interaction in sandy_group.all_interaction_pairs():\n    print(f\'{interaction.person_a.name} + {interaction.person_b.name}\')\n\n# Semantic naming\nprint(sandy_group.semantic_name(heath))  #
